title: Keith M's Fundamental Principles of Information Security
description: A flowchart illustrating the core principles of the CIA triad and their practical applications in network security, showing how they form a foundational framework for protecting information assets.
relationships: Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) are the three core principles.
Authentication and Access Control are key applications that support Confidentiality.
Integrity is maintained through applications like Hashing and Digital Signatures.
Availability is ensured through Redundancy and DDoS Protection.
Non-Repudiation is an application that supports accountability, tying back to Integrity.
